title: Aphanostelma candolleanum
intro: Aphanostelma candolleanum, commonly known as Madagascar periwinkle, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative properties, offering potential health benefits in treating cancer, diabetes, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Aphanostelma candolleanum include vinblastine and vincristine, two alkaloids with significant antitumor activity, as well as other compounds such as ajmalicine, catharanthine, and leucrosine. In herbal medicine, Aphanostelma candolleanum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, although its use is heavily regulated due to its potential toxicity and dependence liability.
taxonomy: Aphanostelma candolleanum belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It is a genus within the Apocynaceae family. The classification of Aphanostelma candolleanum is a part of the larger taxonomic group of flowering plants, specifically within the angiosperm clade.
